projects.name: Ta en öl med YRGO
projects.shortDescription: A mobile social mingel app for YRGO students to connect with the IT companies in Gothenburg and beyond.

descriptionSections.title: Overview
descriptionSections.content: Ta en öl med YRGO is an app designed to facilitate social mingling between YRGO students and IT companies in Gothenburg and beyond. The app allows students and companies to create a personalized beer can with their name and a short description, which can be shared with others to spark conversations and connections in a more personal way. The goal of the app is to create a fun and engaging platform for students and companies to connect, network, and build relationships within the IT industry.

descriptionSections.title: Tech Stack
descriptionSections.content: The mobile app is built with React for the frontend, utilizing Tailwind CSS for styling. The backend is powered by Supabase, which provides a robust and scalable database solution for managing user data and interactions. Vercel BLOB Storage is used to handle image uploads and storage for the personalized beer cans. We have also integrated several npm packages to enhance the functionality and user experience of the app, such as qr-code generation, image cropping and scaling, and design state management.

descriptionSections.title: Process
descriptionSections.content: The development process started with brainstorming together with the digital design students to come up with a unique and engaging concept for the app. We then listed the features we wanted to include and other requirements for the app, as well as limitations and constraints. After that we started the development process by setting up the React frontend and integrating it with the Supabase backend. We implemented the core features of the app, including user authentication, beer can creation, and social sharing functionality. We also integrated Vercel BLOB Storage to handle image uploads for the personalized beer cans.

descriptionSections.title: Results
descriptionSections.content: The app successfully facilitates social mingling between YRGO students and IT companies, providing a platform for meaningful connections and networking opportunities. The personalized beer cans have become a popular feature, encouraging users to engage with each other and share their experiences.

descriptionSections.title: Reflections
descriptionSections.content: Our assigment had very open requirements and we had a lot of freedom in the development process, which was both exciting and challenging. We had to make a lot of decisions along the way, and it was a great learning experience to navigate through the development process with such open-ended requirements. Digital design students were responsible for the design and we were responsible for the development, which was a great way to collaborate and learn from each other. Our idea was ambitious and we had to make some compromises along the way, but we are proud of the final result and the skills we gained throughout the development process!

projects.name: Glas Olsson
projects.shortDescription: A Laravel project for a fictional company with a functional admin panel to manage products and categories.

descriptionSections.title: Overview
descriptionSections.content: A Laravel project crafted by me and my project partner. The goal was to create a functional admin panel for a fictional company where the user can manage products and their categories. From scratch we created a full CRUD system for both products and categories.

descriptionSections.title: Tech Stack
descriptionSections.content: The website is built with Laravel and uses SQlite as the database, following the MVC model for secure routes. Tailwind CSS is used for styling to create a clean and responsive design. The Chart.js library is integrated to visualize sales data and provide insights into product performance.

descriptionSections.title: Process
descriptionSections.content: We started by setting up the Laravel project and designing the database schema for products and categories. We then implemented the CRUD functionality, ensuring that users can easily create, read, update, and delete products and categories. We added a filtering system to allow the user to easily find and manage their products and categories. After that, we focused on styling the admin panel with Tailwind CSS to enhance the user experience. Finally, we integrated Chart.js to display sales data in an interactive and visually appealing way. Throughout the development process, we conducted rigorous testing and debugging to ensure that all features work seamlessly and that the user interface is intuitive and responsive.

descriptionSections.title: Results
descriptionSections.content: The admin panel successfully provides a user-friendly interface for managing products and categories, with all CRUD operations functioning as expected. Our rigerous testing and debugging process ensured a smooth user experience, and the integration of Chart.js allows for insightful data visualization of sales performance. This project pushed my knowlage and showed me how far I've come in just 6 months of learning coding and web development.

descriptionSections.title: Reflections
descriptionSections.content: This project was a great learning experience and provided valuable insights into fullstack develoment with Laravel. It also highlighted the importance of thorough testing and debugging to ensure a smooth user experience.  The effect of scope creep was evident in this project, as we initially had a clear vision but found ourselves adding features and functionality along the way. This was our first major fullstack project, and I am proud of the final result and the skills I gained throughout the development process, and I look forward to applying these skills in future projects.

projects.name: Mos'le Comfortable
projects.shortDescription: A fullstack project for a fictional hotel company called Mos'le Comfortable, featuring a complete hotel booking system with premium services.

descriptionSections.title: Overview
descriptionSections.content: A solo fullstack project for a fictional hotel company called Mos'le Comfortable. The project includes a complete hotel booking system where users can browse available rooms, make reservations, and manage their bookings. Additionally, users can add premium services to their bookings such as pool access and rock climbing. The final product is not optimized for mobile devices as the main focus was on creating a functional and user-friendly booking system.

descriptionSections.title: Tech Stack
descriptionSections.content: The project is built with CSS and javascript for the frontend, while the backend is developed using PHP and SQlite database with Composer for dependency management. While the system was only built with 3 rooms, the application follows a structured architecture to ensure scalability and maintainability.

descriptionSections.title: Process
descriptionSections.content: The goal was to create a functional hotel booking system that allows users to easily browse avaliable rooms, make reservations and add on services if desired. I started by designing the database schema to manage rooms, bookings, and premium services. Then I implemented the backend logic to handle room availability, booking processing, and service management. The frontend was developed to provide an intuitive user interface for browsing rooms, making reservations, and managing bookings. Throughout the development process, I conducted thorough testing and debugging to ensure that all features work seamlessly and that the user experience is smooth and enjoyable.

descriptionSections.title: Results
descriptionSections.content: The hotel booking system provides a smooth experience for users to browse avaliable rooms and make reservations. The backend checks for room avaliability and processes bookings efficiently, while the frontend offers an intuitive interface for managing reservations and adding premium services. This project pushed my knowlage of fullstack development and provided valuable experience in creating a complete web application from scratch.

descriptionSections.title: Reflections
descriptionSections.content: This project was a significant milestone in my learning journey, as it allowed me to apply my knowledge of both frontend and backend development to create a functional web application. While im happy with the final result, I recognize that there are areas for improvement, such as optimizing the application for mobile devices and expanding on the design. Looking back on the code and the development process, I can see how much I've learned and how far I've come and I'm excited to continue building on this foundation and creating even more complex and polished projects in the future.

projects.name: Butter-chicken
projects.shortDescription: A command-line application built with Node.js for finding optimal travel destinations based on budget and current weather.

descriptionSections.title: Overview
descriptionSections.content: A duo created command-line application built with Node.js for finding optimal travel destinations based on value for money and weather conditions. The application allows users to input their weather preferences and target travel region, it then provides recommendations for travel destinations that offer the best value for money while also considering current weather conditions. The goal of this project was to create a useful tool for travelers looking to maximize their travel experience within their budget constraints.

descriptionSections.title: Tech Stack
descriptionSections.content: Built with Node.js and utilizes various NPM packages for handling user input, making API calls, and processing data. The application integrates with external APIs to fetch real-time weather data and currency exchange rates, allowing it to provide accurate and up-to-date recommendations for users.

descriptionSections.title: Process
descriptionSections.content: The development process involved identifying an existing issue and creating a solution for it. We started by designing the application's functionality and user interface, then implemented the core features using Node.js and relevant NPM packages. We integrated external APIs to fetch necessary data for providing travel recommendations, and conducted thorough testing to ensure that the application functions correctly and provides accurate recommendations based on user input.

descriptionSections.title: Results
descriptionSections.content: The command-line application successfully provides users with optimal travel destination recommendations based on their region and weather preferences. The integration of real-time data from external APIs allows for accurate and relevant recommendations, making it a valuable tool for travelers looking to maximize their travel experience within their budget constraints. This project provided valuable experience in building a functional command-line application and working with external APIs in a Node.js environment.

descriptionSections.title: Reflections
descriptionSections.content: This was our first CLI project and it opened our eyes to all the npm packages avaliable for building Node.js applications. We learned how to handle user input in a command-line environment, make chained API calls and error handling in a Node.js application. This project can be futher improved by making a more user-friendly interface by making it a web application instead but for a first project working with Node.js and CLI applications, we are happy with the final result and the skills we gained throughout the development process.
